Belgium’s experienced core, including Kevin De Bruyne and Romelu Lukaku, offers attacking threat but faces an Egypt side organized under Rudi Garcia with strong recent form and set-piece discipline. Limited recent head-to-head encounters and the high-stakes nature of a tournament debut encourage measured first-half approaches from both teams, reducing the likelihood of early dominance. Home-soil travel and standard World Cup pacing further reinforce competitive balance through the opening 45 minutes.
